tasks.directive: tsc reported `TS2724`. Original: '"./spri.service"' has no exported member named 'getCurrentScore'. Did you mean 'getCurrentScores'?

tasks.message: Argument of type 'number' is not assignable to parameter of type '"1H" | "6H" | "24H" | "7D"'.
tasks.directive: Argument type does not match the parameter type. Two common causes: (a) you passed an object where the API expects a sub-property of it (e.g. `getMetaAndAssetCtxs()` returns `{ universe }`, but `fuzzyMatchEntity` expects the bare array — pass `.universe`); (b) the function returns `Promise<T>` and you forgot `await`, so a promise lands where `T` was wanted. Inspect the call site. Original: Argument of type 'number' is not assignable to parameter of type '"1H" | "6H" | "24H" | "7D"'.

tasks.message: Expected 0-1 arguments, but got 2.
tasks.directive: Function called with the wrong number of arguments. EITHER (a) update the call site to match the declared signature exactly, OR (b) widen the function declaration to accept the new positional arguments. Pick ONE — do not rely on `as any`. Original: Expected 0-1 arguments, but got 2.

tasks.message: Type '{ stablecoinId: number & { [CreationAttributeBrand]?: true; }; symbol: string; name: string; compositeScore: number; riskLevel: RiskLevel; dimensionScores: DimensionScores; timestamp: string; }[]' is not assignable to type 'CurrentScoreData[]'.
tasks.directive: Type assignment mismatch. Walk through the source and target types field-by-field — most often a DTO factory built `{ a, b, c }` but the typed target requires `{ a, b, c, d }` (one missing field) or named the field differently (`expiration_date` vs `expiry`). Map field names against the canonical interface in `types/api.ts` (or the equivalent contract type) and patch the factory. Original: Type '{ stablecoinId: number & { [CreationAttributeBrand]?: true; }; symbol: string; name: string; compositeScore: number; riskLevel: RiskLevel; dimensionScores: DimensionScores; timestamp: string; }[]' is not assignable to type 'CurrentScoreData[]'.

tasks.message: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'name' does not exist in type 'CurrentScoreData'.
tasks.directive: Object literal contains a property the target type does not declare. EITHER remove the surplus property, OR add the property to the type (after confirming API_CONTRACTS.json wants it). Never silence with `as any`. Original: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'name' does not exist in type 'CurrentScoreData'.
